summary: Solid soundboard recording of an energetic Texas Fourth‑of‑July show with standout Bird Song, PITB and a smoking second set despite a few lyric slips.
review: The Charlie Miller SBD captures a festive 1981 Austin gig that many fans remember for its high‑octane energy – Jerry’s solos are described as “shredding,” and the second‑set highlights like Bird Song, Playing in the Band and Not Fade Away shine. Sound quality is generally praised as good, though a couple of reviewers note minor imperfections and Bob Weir’s infamous lyric flub on "Leaving Texas".

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating ~3.6 with most reviewers highlighting strong performance and decent sound; weighting show quality (70%) and recording quality (30%) yields a solid 4‑star overall assessment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and balanced SBD; reviewers repeatedly note "good sound" and praise the Miller board, with no major complaints about dropouts or muddiness.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: A lively first set that some felt was uneven, but a fire‑filled second set that kept the crowd moving; occasional lyric slip on "Leaving Texas" is the only noted blemish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers rave about his shredding guitar work and high energy solos.
band_member_comments.Bob: His lyric flub on "Leaving Texas" draws criticism (called a “Bobby blow‑out”), though many fans excuse it as part of the live experience.
